title: Living in Boulder vlog 7 - 28 - 2017: Plattner & Ashby Buildings of Rome Book

enrichment.summary: Kyle records a morning vlog from Boulder, recapping his day: picking up his repaired car (with a broken washer as a side effect), and making progress on a web project cataloging buildings of Rome from the Plattner & Ashby book, organized alphabetically with ~2,000 entries. He expects to finish the project within the week.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Believes his site will be the only interactive directory of ancient Rome buildings of its kind
Wants to finish the Rome book project by end of the week even if it requires extra work
Wishes he had a faster computer earlier in the project

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, V-Log for, I guess, July 28th.
Right now it's 6 in the morning on July 29th.
Um, so yesterday...
Oh, oh, oops, gotta put the, uh, microphone up.
I was up until, like, 5 or something, 5.30, working.
So then I slept in until, like, 1 or something.
And I'm working, and then they called it the car, and they, because it was ready.
And so my mom drove me out.
The car was fixing a couple things, making it look spruced, spruced up.
Get the little caps on the wheels, and the heater thing was pushing out heat, so.
But the guy accidentally, he's a good mechanic, but he accidentally broke my, um, my, uh, my washer.
So now I only have a washer on the left, because it's, like, 500 bucks to fix the one on the right.
So, uh, so I guess I'll have to drop the car price down or whatever, but, um...
Um, anyway, um, so then I came back, and I was working, and, uh, so I finished...
What, which one did I finish?
Um, I finished Justinian Law Code, but I haven't put it on my website yet, and I did.
So I wanted to do, and get back to Blattner and Ashby, the, uh, the book of all the buildings of Rome.
And, um, yeah, so it's organized differently than all my other books, because it's alphabetical, and it has, like, 2,000 buildings.
And, um, so I just went down, and alphabetically just went through the important ones, and created a menu out of that.
And so tomorrow, or I guess today, when I get up, because I'll go to sleep now, even though I'm not tired.
Um, I'm gonna, uh, yeah, you know, drag and drop them in their proper colors, and then make the links.
And then, yeah, I think I'll finish tomorrow, though.
And then I'll put that sucker on my website.
That'll be awesome.
I hope that's, like, the, uh, the directory of ancient Rome.
It's gonna be the only one online.
Um, yeah, and then I'll do, like, a demo of, like, what I've been doing on Google Earth.
I'll do, uh, uh, Baths of Agrippa, downtown Rome.
But the next book I do after this, when I'm done, um, you know, this weekend,
I just want to power through this weekend, but there's a few more.
So, I just want to, but I don't think any of them are time-consuming.
So, I'm gonna go in and, yeah, just tie stuff out.
I mean, I'm so close, you know, even if I have to work in it next week.
So, um, yeah, but, I mean, I'm in no rush to, uh, get my pedals.
That's when I'm gonna ride my bike again, so.
And maybe it'll be another week, you know, so then I'll get all this whole week.
And then I'm done with the whole ancient books.
Pretty much started from scratch, like, a month ago or something.
I wish I had a faster computer before, but, um, anyway.
Um, so, what else?
Oh, yeah, and then more surface-level stuff on my website.
Um, okay, so I think I've talked long enough.
This was the latest or earliest vlog I've ever done.
Six o'clock in the morning.
Peace out.
